Fix false-positive AgentWorking when Copilot remains assigned after finishing (#124)

* Initial plan

* Fix false-positive AgentWorking detection when agent remains assigned after finishing

check_agent_status() previously treated issue assignment as a definitive
signal that the agent was still working. However, Copilot remains assigned
to the issue after finishing, causing false-positive PrCreatedAgentWorking
status.

Add is_pr_work_completed() helper that checks PR-level signals (Copilot
session completion via GraphQL + WIP title fallback) to determine if the
agent is done, overriding the assignment check when PR signals indicate
completion.

commit.message:
Fix airway search: endpoint coordinate filter + wider search box + better logging

Root cause: the SQL query used fragment-level bounding boxes (can span 1000km+)
so segments far from the aircraft were returned; aircraft mid-segment had both
endpoints outside the tiny 78km search box so nothing was ever found.

Changes:
- NavigraphNavDataService: add actual from/to endpoint coordinate filter to SQL
  so only segments with at least one endpoint near the aircraft are returned
- Widen SearchHalfDeg from 0.7/1.0° to 2.5/3.5° (≈278km) to cover mid-segment
  positions (EU airways have 55-185km waypoint spacing)
- Add diagnostic console logging: initial params, chain result per iteration,
  candidate airway name/score, segs scanned — visible in journalctl
- TelegramCommandListener: include NavDataLog in /plot summary response so
  route diagnosis is visible directly in Telegram without checking journalctl

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add GitHub logo to final repo QR slide

commit.message:
fix: iOS rendering with deferred RiveHostView approach

MAUI's layout system sets platform view frames to zero during initial
layout, which prevents RiveView's Metal renderer from initializing
properly. The RiveHostView container defers creating the ViewModel and
RiveView until the container has a valid frame AND is in a window,
mirroring how the native SDK expects views to be set up.

Also removes the CG renderer workaround (no longer needed) and cleans
up the test native button from the sample app.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add envVariables support to all jobs via TrackerParams

Allows per-job environment variable overrides in JSON config params.
Useful for setting different AI models per agent/workflow (Cursor, Copilot, etc).

Changes:
- TrackerParams: add envVariables: Map<String,String> field (common ancestor
  of ALL param classes - Params, JobTrackerParams, etc.)
- PropertyReader: add static ThreadLocal<Map<String,String>> override mechanism
  (setOverrides/clearOverrides) checked FIRST in getValue() — highest priority
- JobRunner.run(): apply envVariables overrides before job execution, always
  clear in finally block (thread-safe, no cross-job contamination)
- Fix TrackerParamsTest: update @AllArgsConstructor call for new field
- Add PropertyReaderOverridesTest: 5 tests for override priority/cleanup
- Add TrackerParamsEnvVariablesTest: 4 tests for JSON deserialization/inheritance

Usage:
  {
    "name": "Expert",
    "params": {
      "envVariables": {
        "OPENAI_MODEL": "gpt-4o",
        "ANTHROPIC_MODEL": "claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022"
      },
      "request": "..."

commit.message:
perf: reduce allocations in token stream formatting

# PR Glass Cockpit

Make review boring. Make truth cheap.

## 💡 Summary
Added explicit capacity hints to String allocations in `tokmd-format`
rendering functions. This avoids repeated memory reallocations when
rendering reports for repositories with many files or languages.

## 🎯 Why (perf bottleneck)
Functions like `render_lang_md`, `render_module_md`, and
`render_diff_md` were initializing with `String::new()` and then
repeatedly extending the string in loops based on row data. This causes
intermediate memory reallocations which hurts runtime performance and
CPU utilization on larger inputs.

## 📊 Proof (before/after)
Performance was measured using `cargo bench -p tokmd-format` with tables
of 1000 rows (`render_lang_tsv_1000`, `render_module_tsv_1000`,
`render_module_md_1000`):

- `render_module_tsv_1000` improved from ~174 µs to ~161 µs (-14.5% to
-1.2%)
- `render_lang_tsv_1000` improved from ~185 µs to ~171 µs (-9.6% to
-7.3%)
- `render_module_md_1000` improved from ~190 µs to ~172 µs (-10.4% to
-9.5%)

## 🧭 Options considered
### Option A (recommended)
- What it is: Use `String::with_capacity` based on a heuristic of rows
and column character count.
- Why it fits this repo: Maintains absolute output determinism, is
highly isolated (SRP), and doesn't change API signatures.
- Trade-offs: Requires a heuristic size which might over-allocate
slightly, but prevents expensive `O(N)` reallocations.

### Option B
- What it is: Refactor formatting to pass a `&mut dyn Write` and stream
directly to output.
- When to choose it instead: If peak memory usage is the critical issue
and allocating the full formatted string is unacceptable.
- Trade-offs: Would require changing API signatures across the
`tokmd-format` crate.

## ✅ Decision
Option A was chosen as it delivers an easy-to-trust performance win
without altering public APIs or structure.

## 🧱 Changes made (SRP)
- `crates/tokmd-format/src/lib.rs`
- Replaced `String::new()` with `String::with_capacity()` using length
heuristics in Markdown and TSV renderers.

## 🧪 Verification receipts
- `cargo bench -p tokmd-format` (verified improvements)
- `cargo build`
- `CI=true cargo test --verbose` (Pass)
- `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` (Pass)
- `cargo fmt -- --check` (Pass)

## 🧭 Telemetry
- Change shape: Shallow string pre-allocation
- Blast radius (API / IO / format stability / concurrency): Purely
internal implementation detail. No API or format changes.
- Risk class + why: Extremely low. Determinism verified by snapshot
tests.
- Rollback: Revert to `String::new()`
- Merge-confidence gates: standard gates.

## 🗂️ .jules updates
- Initialized `.jules/policy/scheduled_tasks.json` and basic `.jules`
runbooks for Bolt.
- Created `20260303T132005Z.json` envelope, `2026-03-03.md` run log, and
updated `ledger.json` in `.jules/bolt/`.

---
*PR created automatically by Jules for task
[9006619675249841418](https://jules.google.com/task/9006619675249841418)
started by @EffortlessSteven*

fix(db): set uor=0 for deprecated care_team layout fields (#10849)

Fresh installs show deprecated `care_team_provider`, `care_team_status`,
and `care_team_facility` DEM layout fields as visible (`uor=1`), while
the 7.0.3→7.0.4 upgrade script correctly hides them (`uor=0`). This
inconsistency means fresh installs and upgraded installs behave
differently for fields that have been superseded by the
`care_team_member` table. Additionally, users who installed fresh on
8.0.0 will have these fields set incorrectly and need a remediation path
via the upgrade script.

## Changes

- **`sql/database.sql`**: Set `uor=0` on the three deprecated
`care_team_*` `layout_options` INSERT statements, aligning fresh
installs with the state produced by the upgrade path. Incremented the
`v_database` marker to 535.
- **`version.php`**: Incremented `$v_database` from 534 to 535 to stay
in sync with the `database.sql` marker.
- **`sql/8_0_0-to-8_0_1_upgrade.sql`**: Added a `#IfRow3D`-guarded
UPDATE to set `uor=0` for the three deprecated `care_team_*` fields,
ensuring users who installed fresh on 8.0.0 (and thus have `uor=1`) are
corrected when upgrading to 8.0.1.

```sql
-- Before (database.sql)
VALUES ('DEM', 'care_team_provider', ..., 1, ...)
VALUES ('DEM', 'care_team_status',   ..., 1, ...)
VALUES ('DEM', 'care_team_facility', ..., 1, ...)

-- After (database.sql)
VALUES ('DEM', 'care_team_provider', ..., 0, ...)
VALUES ('DEM', 'care_team_status',   ..., 0, ...)
VALUES ('DEM', 'care_team_facility', ..., 0, ...)

-- Added to 8_0_0-to-8_0_1_upgrade.sql
UPDATE `layout_options` SET `uor` = 0
  WHERE `form_id` = 'DEM'
  AND `field_id` IN ('care_team_facility', 'care_team_provider', 'care_team_status')
  AND `uor` = 1;
```

Symlink ~/Downloads to /downloads and pre-create Telegram Desktop subfolder

- entrypoint.sh: symlinks /home/gwb/Downloads -> /downloads so Telegram's
  default download path writes directly to the mounted volume. Also creates
  /downloads/Telegram Desktop/ to avoid Mp3tag 'Select Folder' dialog.
- run-all.sh: Mp3tag now opens Z:\downloads\Telegram Desktop as default
  folder (matching where Telegram actually saves files).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
